question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the delivery of the Mental Health Strategy 2021-2031.
answer_text: Delivery of the Mental Health Strategy has been taking place within an extremely challenging financial context using existing, limited resources to take forward actions. As a result of this, the Strategy is being implemented through Annual Delivery Plans which set year-on-year priorities and actions. Good progress has been made in taking forward a number of actions, including:Implementation of the community perinatal mental health service across the region, with teams now in place in all Trusts.Co-design of a three-year implementation plan for the establishment of the Regional Mental Health Service (RMHS). Completion of a three-year Early Intervention and Prevention Action Plan, with progress made on implementation of actions.Completion and publication of an independent Mental Health workforce review, with work underway to begin planning implementation of key recommendations.   Additional investment in Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) to enhance existing provision and alleviate pressures.Roll-out of the emotional health and wellbeing teams in schools, with initial cohort of schools identified and engaged.Establishment of the Digital Mental Health Board and appointment of project leads to develop a digital mental health service action plan.  Completion of a review exploring the optimal approach to engaging the community and voluntary sector in the development of mental health policy and provision.The Annual Delivery Plan for 2024/25 is currently in development and will be co-produced through engagement with a wide range of stakeholders. This Plan will set out an agreed set of actions which will be taken forward in 2024/25.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for his assessment of the potential closure of the Belfast Metropolitan College, Castlereagh East Campus, and (ii) when he will meet with management to discuss the current consultation.
answer_text: I understand that Belfast Metropolitan College is currently running a public consultation on the potential closure of its Castlereagh campus. This consultation exercise enables stakeholders to have their voices heard and contribute valuable insights and evidence to the decision-making process.When the consultation concludes, it will be a matter for the College to consider all representations made and decide on next steps. I will be seeking an update on the position in relation to the decision when the consultation concludes; it would be inappropriate for me to comment further while that consultation is ongoing.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will ensure that the Active Schools Travel Programme will continue to be funded by his department in 2024-25.
answer_text: The Active School Travel programme is jointly funded by my Department and the Public Health Agency. The programme is currently being delivered under contract with Sustrans which is due to expire on 31 July 2024. My officials are engaging with the Public Health Agency on the way forward, and any decision on these programmes in the next financial year will be subject to the availability of funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how much of the Belfast Cycling Network Delivery Plan has been completed in the last 3 years, broken down by year.
answer_text: The Belfast Cycle Network Delivery Plan (BCN) was published in March 2022.  The typical design period for a scheme of the nature within the plan is around two years, and often involves engagement with the public on emerging options. This is generally followed by statutory processes to legislate a cycle route before the scheme can proceed to construction. In 2023 sections of the Forth Meadow Community Greenway were delivered in partnership with Belfast City Council and an innovative, environmentally friendly, street lighting scheme along the urban section of the Comber Greenway was also completed. My officials are currently developing the majority of the projects on the BCN short-term scheme list, covering the period 2022 – 2025 and aim to bring a number of these projects forward for public consultation and then construction in 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what plans he has to introduce an enhanced flood warning system in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The need to provide information around possible flood warnings is becoming more important given the impacts of climate change. Whilst my Department believes that the North would benefit from an enhanced flood forecasting system, it is at present not currently funded to provide this service.  Whilst there would be finance required to establish and maintain such a system, there are other factors to be considered to ensure that any flood forecasting system here reflects our unique river catchment status whilst building on the already well-established strong network of community resilience groups.My officials have already had initial discussions with other organisations who have flood risk management responsibilities in Britain, and who have already developed their own forecasting systems, to ascertain if a similar approach could be developed for the North.  This is at an early stage and is estimated that the costs would be approximately £2m per year with around three years to develop.  In the meantime, my Department will continue to work with multi-agency partners, including Local Government, to provide support to communities at risk of flooding and to help them prepare for, and to respond to all weather related emergencies.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what plans he has to increase the funding for Palforzia, to enable increased access to the drug for children with severe nut allergies.
answer_text: Officers within the Department and its SPPG remain committed to the introduction of a local service to provide Palforzia to patients within Northern Ireland.  Work is ongoing to agree the most appropriate service delivery model for Northern Ireland.  In the interim the regime is available on case by case basis for eligible patients in line with the Department’s endorsed guidance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what funding opportunities are currently available for tennis clubs in East Belfast.
answer_text: Information on this scheme can be found on Sport NI’s website at: http://www.sportni.net/funding/supporting-sport-to-build-back-better/project-re-boot-activate/Sport NI regularly publish on its website www.sportni.net/funding/

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what plans she has to review, or increase, the Education Maintenance Allowance payment.
answer_text: The Education Maintenance Allowance (EMA) is a joint Department for the Economy and Department of Education Scheme.  There are no plans to increase the current allowance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his officials have explored adding the Comber Greenway to the winter gritting schedule for (i) 2022; and (ii) 2023.
answer_text: I have confirmed my commitment to connecting communities and encouraging more active travel and have also indicated that developing a safer, cleaner travel culture is central to my key priorities. I want to carefully consider how best to deliver on my active and sustainable travel ambitions and part of that consideration should involve how we improve conditions for walking, cycling and using public transport in the winter months. The Comber Greenway is a real asset and I am delighted that its popularity with walkers, wheelers and cyclists continues to grow.  However coming up with a solution that would allow it and other greenways and footways to be treated in icy weather does present some significant logistical, financial and resource challenges which, with so many other competing pressures, my Department is unable to take forward at this time. Should you have any further questions on this please contact DfI Roads Southern Division at dfiroads.southern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what plans there are to improve cycling infrastructure on the route between the Castlereagh Road and the Cregagh Road.
answer_text: The Belfast Cycle Network Delivery Plan (BCNDP) contains a large number of schemes that are intended to encourage cycling in the city and reduce the dependency on the use of the private car.  My officials are currently developing a number of schemes within the BCNDP for inclusion in a short term plan for delivery between 2022 and 2025.This includes a scheme on Montgomery Road and officials have recently carried out an informal consultation to allow designs to be finalised with a view to formal consultation taking place early in 2023.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Eastern Division at: dfiroads.eastern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) to outline what measures his Department is taking to replace European Social Funding and protect vital services; and (ii) for his assessment of the impact that the loss of this funding will have on health service provision.
answer_text: It is not within the remit of the Department of Health or Trusts to commission services that solely pursue employment or training outcomes. Therefore, the Department does not have the power to replace funding lost from the cessation of the European Social Fund. Health and Social Trusts do commission Day Opportunities for people with disabilities, which may include educational and employability activities to pursue health outcomes. The Department fully recognises the importance of employment and meaningful day opportunities to improve health and wellbeing outcomes, both in reducing poverty and providing the opportunity for people to reach their full potential.I am aware that the European Social Fund (ESF) concludes in March 2023 with several projects receiving match funding from Health & Social Care Trusts. While details of the Shared Prosperity Fund are yet to be shared with NI Executive Departments, there is likely to be a significant shortfall in funding, which may impact the level of provision and sustainability of projects. My officials are liaising with Health and Social Care Trusts to fully assess the potential impact on health service provision. Further engagement with HSC Trusts is required to identify vulnerable service users and develop individual contingency plans if ESF funded projects become unsustainable. The outputs of this exercise will form part of an Impact Assessment, to be shared with the Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities (DLUHC) to influence the scope of the Shared Prosperity Fund and future commissioning. Departmental officials engaged in a cross-departmental meeting with DLUHC on 14 October 2022, to raise the profile of ESF projects in Northern Ireland and to re-iterate concerns on the Shared Prosperity Fund.Officials continue to liaise with the Departments for Economy, Finance and Communities to identify potential solutions, cognisant that government is operating in a challenging financial context and in the absence of an Executive. In addition, my officials engage directly with independent sector provides to explore sustainability challenges and provide updates on departmental actions. I can assure you that Ministers across departments are committed to ensuring that work continues at an official level to maximise the benefit of the Shared Prosperity Fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on Blue Green Infrastructure projects in East Belfast.
answer_text: I am keen to support sustainable transport options that connect communities, tackle the impact of climate change and improve lives.  A number of blue green infrastructure projects have been completed in East Belfast with several others currently planned.Earlier this year my Department widened a portion of the Comber Greenway from Billy Neil Playing Fields to the Enler River Bridge.  As part of this project, maintenance work adjacent to the Enler River Bridge was also completed.My Department has also completed tree and shrub planting at the following locations:•	Comber Greenway (additional sites to those already completed in 2021);•	Armitage Close, Sydenham; and•	Carncaver Road, Lisnasharragh.A number of small-scale accessible dropped kerb schemes have been identified in the East Belfast area including; Belmont Road and Dundela Area, Euston Street, Inverary Avenue, Tamery Pass and Montrose Street South.  These are due to be installed during the 2022/23 financial year.Proposals for a Sydenham Greenway have undergone public consultation and my officials are currently finalising the Consultation report.Informal consultation on the proposed improvements to the Cycle route on Island Street is now complete.  Work on finalising the design is underway and officials plan to formally consult on proposals in early 2023.Similarly, my officials have completed informal consultation on the proposed Cycle route on Montgomery Road.  Work on finalising the design is underway and officials plan to formally consult on proposals in early 2023.It is also planned to provide a link from Millars Lane to the Comber Greenway in the 2023/24 financial year.My Department is currently preparing a feasibility study to enhance the Ravenhill Road section of the Belfast Cycling Network from the Albertbridge Road to the junction with the Ormeau Road.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what support her Department provides for artists seeking studio space in Belfast.
answer_text: The Department’s principal avenue of support to the arts is delivered via Arts Council.  Arts Council run a number of funding schemes with the potential to support organisations and individuals with the costs associated with studio space.  This includes but is not limited to its Annual Funding Programme which can support organisations with core running costs, including rental of studio space.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what support is available for those living with long COVID.
answer_text: The following services to support and assist people who are living with this condition have been established in all 5 HSC Trusts.Strand 1 -  Post-COVID-19 syndrome (PCS) patients referred by primary or secondary care to a one-stop-shop MDT assessment servicePatients can be referred to the MDT assessment clinic from primary and secondary care.  Patients receive an holistic assessment to include:identifying patients with breathlessness who may benefit from pulmonary rehabilitation; and, those who may need onward referral to secondary or community services and/or signposting to self-management resources.Strand 2: - Bespoke pulmonary rehabilitation / dysfunctional breathing service for patients with significant respiratory symptoms post-COVID-19The purpose of this strand is to put in place timely, effective arrangements for outpatient rehabilitation following COVID-19 resulting in significant respiratory symptoms.  Strand 3 - Patients discharged from critical care (both COVID-19 and non-COVID-19)The purpose of this strand is to put in place timely, effective  arrangements for the follow-up and assessment of people discharged from critical care (both COVID-19 and non-COVID-19), reflecting the known risk of long-term sequelae following a long stay in critical care.Strand 4 - Strengthening psychology support to all TrustsThe psychological impact of COVID-19 (depression, anxiety disorders, PTSD, traumatic bereavement, psychosis, risk to self and/or others) is recognised in extant national guidance.  Each Trust has been supported to recruit an additional senior psychologist, with their main role supporting strand 3, but also providing advice to staff working in strands 1 and 2.Strand 5 - Regional MDT clinical physiology, SLT and dietetic support Selected patients may need diagnostic tests by respiratory clinical physiologists, onward referral for dietetic and speech and language therapy (SLT) from the one-stop-shop MDT assessment service, the critical care follow-up and pulmonary rehabilitation strands.  Strand 6 - Signposting and access to self-management resourcesPatients in NI can access self-management resources which include the NHS App Your Covid Recovery (Managing The Effects | Your COVID Recovery)

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether his Department plans to extend the current rates holiday for small businesses.
answer_text: There are no plans to extend the rates holiday for small businesses given that it is not possible to bring forward any additional or extended rates support in the absence of an Executive.However, I have extended the Small Business Rate Relief scheme into the 2022/23 rating year, which will directly benefit 29,000 small businesses, and I have frozen the regional rate for business ratepayers for the second year in a row.  This is in addition to the £50 million of support delivered through the rates holiday period for this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how her Department is measuring and monitoring (i) air quality; and (ii) air COVID-19 safety in schools.
answer_text: CO2 monitors have been provided to all schools in Northern Ireland. The CO2 monitors measure the efficacy of ventilation in a classroom and provide feedback to the school as to how effective the ventilation is within that classroom. This will allow schools to identify any areas where ventilation may need to be improved, and highlight these to the Education Authority (EA). CO2 monitors do not provide evidence on the presence of COVID-19 in the air.If a school has concerns regarding ventilation the EA Maintenance Service has established a Ventilation Remedial Team which will prioritise site visits to those schools where ventilation concerns have been reported to the EA Maintenance Helpdesk. If natural ventilation is proven to be deficient within a school, remedial works will be undertaken to rectify the situation which may include repairs or replacement of windows or the installation of an air ventilation system.Expert advice from the Scientific Advisory Group for Emergencies and Health and Safety Executive Northern Ireland continues to be that air filtration units should not be used as a substitute for ventilation.  In line with this advice, air filtration units will only be supplied to schools by EA Maintenance as an interim measure, where immediate remedial works cannot improve the efficacy of ventilation in certain rooms or there is a lead in time for the completion of the works.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether she has met with the Minister of Health to discuss how her Department and the Department of Health could work together to minimise the spread of COVID-19 in schools.
answer_text: My Department and the Department of Health continue to work together to ensure the effects of the pandemic are minimised for our children and school staff. The ongoing co-operation between our two Departments has allowed various strategies to be implemented to combat COVID-19.The most effective mitigating measure in relation to Covid-19 is natural ventilation and my Department and the Education Authority will support schools in prioritising the effective implementation of this measure.My Departmental officials, along with their counterparts in the Education Authority (EA), have engaged with colleagues in the Department of Health to establish a Ventilation Working Group and a Ventilation Remedial Works Team is in place to identify deficiencies and take appropriate remedial action.My officials will continue to work closely with colleagues in the Department of Health to minimise the spread of COVID-19 in schools.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) how many people have long Covid; and (ii) what plans his Department has to support and assist people who are living with the condition.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what research his Department has undertaken into the potential of Car Club vehicles in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My Department is currently considering how cleaner transport, new business models and new modes of travel can transform how people, goods and services move, as part of our work in developing options for inclusion in our new suite of Transport Plans.I believe that the greater use of more efficient, on-demand transport schemes, such as Car Clubs, would help enable better integrated services, helping to reduce emissions and congestion in our towns and cities. Virtually all Car Clubs in other jurisdictions operate as commercial businesses, and as such my Department has no plans to actively establish or operate them. Members of the public already have access to Car Club vehicles in several urban areas across the North. Additionally, in consultation with my Department, a number of developers have recently included Car Clubs in their Travel Plans for residential developments in Belfast.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department plans to transfer control of pavements to local councils.
answer_text: My Department does not currently have any plans to transfer the management and operation of pavements to local councils.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether her Department plans to transfer regeneration powers to local councils.
answer_text: Previous attempts to transfer further regeneration and community development powers to local locals have not succeeded.Due to the significant period of time, which has passed since the introduction of the last Regeneration Bill (2014), and the experience of recent initiatives in response to Covid-19 e.g. the Covid-19 Recovery Revitalisation and Small Settlements Programmes in partnership with DfI and DAERA, the cross-departmental nature of regeneration activities has been clearly demonstrated.This confirms the need for Executive consideration of this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how his Department encourages active travel for staff within his Department.
answer_text: Staff are regularly encouraged to participate in active travel initiatives through my Department’s internal communications channels. This includes the ‘Leading the Way’ initiative which is supported by the Public Health Agency and delivered by Sustrans. This encourages DfI staff to attend walks, cycling events and workshops to support their active travel choices.  My Department is also committed to the annual 4-week Active Travel Challenge (ATC), to encourage people to leave the car at home and choose more active modes of travel. There are various bikes available, folding and standard, at the Department’s headquarters in Clarence Court for staff use for attendance at meetings and leisure use at lunchtime as well as changing rooms, showering facilities and up to 70 secure bike parking spaces available.  As well as being taking part in the employer Cycle to Work scheme that allows staff to purchase bikes through salary sacrifice, my Department also has received Silver accreditation from Cycling UK, an international recognition as a cycle friendly employer, showing a commitment to enhancing cycling opportunities for staff and visitors.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of people living with COPD in (i) Northern Ireland; and, (ii) East Belfast.
answer_text: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) is one of the disease registers in the Quality and Outcomes Framework (QOF); QOF disease prevalence data is published annually and the COPD register contains the number of patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.  As at 31st March 2022, there were 41,283 people on the COPD register for Northern Ireland.  QOF disease prevalence data is also available and published at GP Federation level; at 31st March 2022, there were 2,513 people on the COPD register for the East Belfast GP Federation.Further information on QOF disease prevalence data is available at:https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/articles/prevalence-statistics.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of people living with asthma in (i) Northern Ireland; and, (ii) East Belfast.
answer_text: Asthma is one of the disease registers in the Quality and Outcomes Framework (QOF); QOF disease prevalence data is published annually and the Asthma register contains the number of patients with asthma, excluding those who have had no prescription for asthma-related drugs in the last 12 months.  As at 31st March 2022, there were 126,442 people on the Asthma register for Northern Ireland.  QOF disease prevalence data is also available and published at GP Federation level; at 31st March 2022, there were 7,237 people on the Asthma register for the East Belfast GP Federation.Further information on QOF disease prevalence data is available at:https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/articles/prevalence-statistics.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) when the Review of Neurology Services will be published; and, (ii) what resources and staffing have been allocated to the completion and production of the review.
answer_text: The Regional Review of Neurology Services is tasked with identifying the optimum configuration of neurology services for the next 10-15 years. A Neurology Review Team was established to take the Review forward, with membership from the Medical, Nursing and AHP workforces, the NI Neurological Charities Alliance, Primary Care and Commissioning.Completion of the review is subject to available clinical capacity. In recognition of this pressure, the Department recently secured protected time for a number of members of the Review Team. It is envisaged that this protected time will enable the development of emerging recommendations for improvement by early autumn.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) what function Section 25 of the Northern Ireland (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 2006 performs within his Department; and (ii) with whom enforcement of the provision currently lies.
answer_text: i) Section 25 of the Northern Ireland (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 2006 places a statutory duty on all Departments and Councils to promote the achievement of sustainable development in Northern Ireland.  In performing this requirement my Department undertakes a wide range of actions including policy development, programme/project delivery and funding support for those areas which fall under my Department’s remit and in doing so seeks to further the delivery of the 2030 UN Agenda for Sustainable Development. Due to the breadth and depth of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and vast range of work DAERA undertakes I will provide a few high level examples of how my Department performs this function. Areas of delivery include the introduction of new NI Climate Change legislation, the protection of natural habitats and species (terrestrial, freshwater and marine) through nature conservation designations and funding support, support to rural communities, promotion and support for sustainable agriculture, provision of education and monitoring drinking water quality amongst many other areas. My Department has also worked to integrate the delivery of the SDGs into high level Government policy/strategy. In particular the recent draft NI Programme for Government and the draft Green Growth Strategy. ii) There are no provisions in the Northern Ireland (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 2006 to allow for enforcement by any NI Department.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether proposals to assist buildings affected by unsafe non-ACM cladding can be progressed in the absence of an Executive.
answer_text: As detailed in my response to your related question (AQW 380/22-27) work is ongoing to progress proposals for a remediation scheme for unsafe non-ACM cladding in buildings over 18 metres.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when proposals to assist buildings affected by unsafe non-ACM cladding will be finalised and published.
answer_text: My Department is continuing to scope a non-ACM scheme with support from the Department of Finance.		DfC are engaging with officials in the Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities on this.  		In the meantime, details of all of the properties that were the subject of submissions to the DoF call for evidence have been shared by DoF with the Fire and Rescue Service, who have asked for additional information on buildings names and confirmed heights. They will consider buildings within scope and work to adapt response plans for the intention of mitigating associated risk.
